name: Montana

examFacts.notes: Administered by Pearson VUE for the Montana Board of Realty Regulation (within the Department of Labor and Industry's Business Standards Division). The 80-question national portion and the 40-question Montana state-law portion are scored separately, each requiring a scaled score of 75.

intro:
Montana scores its national and 40-question state-law portions separately, so a strong national score won't carry a thin grasp of Montana rules. The Montana Board of Realty Regulation, which sits within the Department of Labor and Industry's Business Standards Division, contracts with Pearson VUE to deliver the test as 120 scored questions split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Montana state-law portion, with two and a half hours allowed for the national questions and an hour and a half for the state questions. The two portions are scored separately on a scaled 0–100 scale and you need a 75 on each, so a strong national score won't carry you if your grasp of Montana law is thin. That is why Montana-specific preparation matters as much as the national material.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. Montana tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the Title 37 Chapter 51 brokerage relationships law that lets a licensee act as a statutory broker without becoming the agent of either party, to the mandatory relationship disclosure required at the first reasonable opportunity, the Small Tract Financing Act trust indenture and 120-day non-judicial trustee's sale that replaces the ordinary mortgage foreclosure on tracts of 40 acres or less, Montana's prior-appropriation water rights and the way water rights pass with the land, the Real Estate Recovery Account that pays consumer judgments when a licensee can't, and an errors-and-omissions insurance mandate that every active licensee must satisfy every year. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Montana-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Montana real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Montana bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real Board of Realty Regulation practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Montana question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: The Montana Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 120 scored multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Montana state-law portion. Pearson VUE delivers both portions in one computer-based session and adds five unscored pretest questions on the national portion and up to ten on the state portion. The pretest questions look just like the scored ones, so the time spent on them does not count against your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: The two portions are scored separately on a scaled 0–100 scale, and you need a 75 on each. Scaling means your raw score is statistically adjusted for the difficulty of the specific form you took, so 75 scaled is not the same as 75 percent correct. If you pass one portion and fail the other you only retake the portion you failed, and your authorization to test stays valid for up to five years after you finished the first required pre-licensing course.

faq.q: How much does the Montana real estate exam cost?
faq.a: Pearson VUE charges $95 to sit the salesperson exam, and that one fee covers both portions when they are scheduled together. If you fail one portion and have to retake only that portion, the retake is another $95. Vouchers are sold through Pearson VUE if a school or employer is paying. Fees are non-refundable and non-transferable except under the published change and cancel policy.

faq.q: How long is the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours of seat time total. Pearson VUE allows two and a half hours for the 80-question national portion and an hour and a half for the 40-question Montana portion. The two portions are scheduled separately but can be taken back-to-back in one sitting, and your unofficial pass or fail report is provided before you leave. Pearson VUE runs test centers in Montana's larger cities and also offers the exam through its OnVUE online proctoring platform from home.

faq.q: Who administers the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: The Montana Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by Pearson VUE for the Montana Board of Realty Regulation. You register and schedule your exam date through Pearson VUE.

faq.q: Is the Montana real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It's a serious test, and the part that catches people off guard is the 40-question Montana portion. The national half covers the same principles taught in every state, but the state questions reward candidates who actually studied Montana's brokerage relationships law, the relationship disclosure form, the statutory broker concept, the Small Tract Financing Act, and Montana's errors-and-omissions insurance rules. Most people who fail leaned too heavily on national material and underprepared on Montana law. Honest reps on Montana-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a $95 retake.

faq.q: What's on the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers real property characteristics and legal descriptions, forms of ownership and transfer of title, property value and appraisal, real estate contracts and agency, the practice of real estate including fair housing, property disclosures and environmental issues, financing and settlement, and real estate math. Contracts and agency together carry the most weight, with sixteen items on the national outline. The 40-question Montana portion covers the Board of Realty Regulation's investigatory and disciplinary powers, licensing activities and license maintenance, the substantive requirements that govern the day-to-day activities of licensees including advertising, broker–salesperson supervision, unprofessional conduct, compensation, agency and property disclosure, handling documents and handling monies, and additional topics like errors-and-omissions insurance, common-interest ownership, the Residential Landlord and Tenant Act, and foreclosure and redemption. Activities of licensees is by far the heaviest state topic with 28 of the 40 items, so it is worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish Montana's required pre-licensing education, the highest-leverage move is grinding Montana-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question state portion rewards repetition on the brokerage relationships law, the relationship disclosure form, the statutory broker concept, trust account rules, and the Small Tract Financing Act foreclosure process. The RealReady app gives you the full Montana question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you are weak.

The 20 Montana practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Montana bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Montana real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Montana question bank built for the Montana real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Montana law that trips people up, like the Title 37 Chapter 51 statutory broker relationship, the Small Tract Financing Act trust indenture, and the Real Estate Recovery Account.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Montana's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
